title: Do-no-harm / privacy & sensitive-feature screen (blocking)
context: Visibility is not always good. Mapping the settlements, shelters, or movement of a persecuted or displaced group can expose them to harm. OSM's humanitarian mapping community (HOT, Missing Maps) has codified do-no-harm practice to address this. open-map-gaps must operationalize this as a non-skippable blocking gate with a named reviewer who can restrict the feature set, lower geo-precision, or exclude an area entirely. Any sensitive or conflict-zone context escalates to riskTier:high requiring expert/ethics sign-off. No pack may be mapped until the do-no-harm screen is completed and signed for that pack.
objective: Design and commit a do-no-harm / privacy screen rubric that serves as a mandatory blocking gate for every pack, with decision options, escalation rules, PII prohibitions, and a reviewer-signed artifact template.
acceptanceCriteria: A rubric assessing whether mapping the area/feature set could expose vulnerable people (persecuted/displaced groups, shelters, safe houses, sensitive infrastructure) is committed.
Decision options are defined: PROCEED / RESTRICT feature set / LOWER geo-precision / EXCLUDE; default to caution on any doubt.
Escalation rule is defined: conflict/vulnerable-population contexts require riskTier:high and expert/ethics sign-off before mapping begins.
Enforces the people-are-not-features rule: no occupant/household/individual data; partner-side de-identification and consent required for any field data.
Produces a committed, reviewer-signed do-no-harm artifact template per pack achieving 100% coverage of all packs.
Reviewed and signed by the do-no-harm reviewer before any pack may be mapped.
No OSM write credentials are requested or stored; this task produces documentation only.
output: A committed do-no-harm screen rubric and artifact template covering: vulnerability assessment checklist, decision options (PROCEED/RESTRICT/LOWER/EXCLUDE), escalation rules for high-risk contexts, PII/people-are-not-features prohibitions, and a reviewer-sign-off field — ready to be completed per pack.
